name: Guduchi
usesInAyurveda: Tridoshic Modulation: Powerful Tridoshahara (pacifies all three doshas), specifically excels in balancing Pitta and Kapha. While its Tikta (bitter) and Kashaya (astringent) Rasa and Ushna (heating) Virya help in clearing Kapha, its post-digestive Madhura (sweet) Vipaka and Guna (qualities) help in nourishing Vata and Pitta. It is the ultimate 'Sama' (balanced) herb.
Karma (Actions): Rasayana (rejuvenative), Sangrahi (absorbent), Balya (strength-promoting), Deepana (appetizer), Pachana (digestive), Jwarahara (antipyretic), and Vishaghna (anti-toxic).

API/AFI Adherence: The dried stem is the official medicinal part as per the Ayurvedic Pharmacopoeia of India (API). It is the central ingredient in almost all classical rejuvenative and immunity tonics.
Specific Indications: Used in Jwara (Fever/Chronic infections), Pandu (Anemia), Kamala (Jaundice), Amavata (Rheumatoid arthritis), Prameha (Diabetes), and Kustha (Skin diseases).
usageForm: Stem juice (Swarasa), Stem powder (Churna), Stem decoction (Kwatha), Water-soluble extract (Guduchi Satva), Fermented extract (Arishta).
mainMedicinalUses: Curing the Root (Amrita): Known as 'Amrita' (the nectar of immortality). It is the premier Ayurvedic immunomodulator, enhancing the body's natural defense (Ojas) against pathogens and auto-immune disorders. WHO-aligned research supports its role in stimulating macrophage activity.

Antipyretic Excellence: Highly effective as a Jwarahara (fever reducer), particularly in chronic (Jirna) and low-grade fevers. It helps in clearing the system of 'Ama' (metabolic waste) which is the root cause of infections. It is a key herb for managing dengue and malaria recovery.

Liver and Spleen Support: Its Tikta (bitter) and Ushna (heating) properties help in liver detoxification and healthy bile secretion. It is traditionally used to treat jaundice and fatty liver by reducing inflammatory load and regenerating hepatocytes.

Anti-Arthritic and Gout Care: Its ability to clear uric acid and balance Vata makes it a supreme choice for Gout (Vata-rakta) and Rheumatoid Arthritis (Amavata). It reduces joint inflammation and improves mobility through its systemic 'Lekhana' (scraping) effect.

Diabetes and Metabolic Support: Its Tikta (bitter) and Kashaya (astringent) properties help in regulating blood sugar levels and improving insulin sensitivity. It helps in reducing the oxidative stress associated with chronic metabolic diseases.

Stress and Cognitive Health: Acts as an adaptogen that modulates the stress hormone cortisol. Its 'Rasayana' (rejuvenative) quality provides a systemic calming effect on the nervous system, helping to manage panic attacks and anxiety.

Phytochemical Profile: Rich in Terpenoids (Tinosporoside, Columbin), Alkaloids (Berberine, Palmatine), Flavonoids, and Polysaccharides. These compounds contribute to its potent immunomodulatory and antioxidant properties.
plantProfile: Unique History: Classified under the 'Vayah-sthapana' (age-stabilizing) and 'Rasayana' (rejuvenative) groups in ancient Ayurvedic treatises like Charaka Samhita. The name 'Guduchi' signifies its ability to 'protect the entire body'. Historically, it is associated with the celestial 'Amrita' (nectar) that fell from the sky during the battle between gods and demons.

Physical Characteristics: A large, deciduous, woody, succulent, climbing shrub. The leaves are heart-shaped (cordate), entire, and long-stalked. The flowers are small, yellowish-green, in axillary pseudoracemes. The fruit is an ovoid drupe, turning red when ripe. It produces characteristic long, slender, aerial roots. It is found throughout tropical India and Southeast Asia.

Specific Chemical Compounds: Primarily contains Terpenoids (Tinosporoside, Cordifolioside A-E), Alkaloids (Berberine, Palmatine, Tinosporin), and Glycosides. The presence of tinosporoside is crucial for its anti-viral and anti-inflammatory action on the immune system.

Strict Standardizations: The stem is standardized in API based on its greyish-brown or creamy bark and bitter taste. Physicochemical benchmarks: total ash (<7%), alcohol-soluble extractive (>3%), and water-soluble extractive (>10%). HPLC is used to monitor tinosporoside and columbin content.
preparationMethods: Stem Decoction: 20g of stem boiled in 160ml water reduced to 40ml for chronic fevers and immunity support. Take twice daily before meals.
Guduchi Satva: The water-soluble starch extracted from the stem; taken (500mg - 1g) for Pitta-related burning sensations and liver issues.
Fresh Juice: 10-20ml of fresh stem juice taken twice daily with honey (for Kapha) or ginger water (for Vata) for maximum absorption.
Amrita-Arishta: 15-30ml of fermented extract taken twice daily after meals for managing chronic infections and joint pain.
Stem Powder: 3-6g of fine powder taken twice daily with warm water or milk for general health and vitality.

dosages.Children: Generally safe. 2-5 ml of fresh juice or 1g of powder mixed with honey twice daily for enhancing general immunity and growth.
dosages.Adults: Powder: 3-6 grams; Decoction: 20-50 ml; Satva: 500-1000 mg; Juice: 10-20 ml, divided throughout the day before meals.
dosages.Elderly: Ideal for age-related immunity support and preventing bone loss. Dosage: 3g of powder daily with warm water at bedtime.

safetyProfile: Contraindications: Generally very safe. However, individuals with very low blood sugar should use it in moderation as it has a systemic blood-sugar-lowering effect. No severe contraindications recorded in WHO monographs.
GRAS Status: Classified as a safe medicinal herb. It has been used for millennia across various traditional systems (Ayurveda, Unani, and TCM) without documented toxicity.

Herb-Drug Interactions: Monitor if taken alongside synthetic anti-diabetic or immunosuppressant medications as it may potentiate or interfere with their effects. No severe adverse interactions reported in clinical literature.
Adverse Effects: None documented at traditional therapeutic levels. Excessive use in very sensitive individuals may rarely cause mild skin cooling or stomach dryness.
